name: Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Crust: Process Oreo cookies into crumbs in a food processor with the metal blade. Add melted butter and mix until well combined. Alternatively, blend crumbs and butter in a bowl until smooth. Pour the mixture into a 10-inch springform pan and press evenly over the bottom and about two-thirds up the sides. Refrigerate.
2. Melt chocolate and peanut butter chips in a double boiler over simmering water. Gradually add heavy cream and stir until smooth and chips are melted. Pour the mixture into the crust-lined pan and spread to within 1/2 inch of the sides. Return to refrigerator.
3. Filling: In a large bowl, beat cream cheese and sugar with an electric mixer on medium speed until light and fluffy (about 3 minutes). Stop occasionally to scrape down the sides of the bowl and beaters. Add peanut butter and mix until incorporated. Beat in eggs and egg white one at a time, scraping down the sides several times. Add sour cream, lemon juice, and chocolate chips, mixing until combined. Pour into the prepared pan.
4. Place on a baking sheet. Bake at 325°F (165°C) for 55 to 65 minutes or until the edges are firm and the center jiggles slightly. Remove from wire rack and cool in a draft-free place for 15 minutes. Increase oven temperature to 350°F (175°C).
5. Topping: Melt chocolate chips in a double boiler over simmering water. Remove from heat, then add sour cream and sugar, stirring until smooth. Spread evenly over the top of the cake. Return to oven for 10 minutes or until set. Cool to room temperature, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate at least 6 hours or until chilled. Before serving, remove sides of the springform pan.
Note: The cake can be stored in refrigerator up to 3 days or frozen in the springform pan covered with plastic wrap and foil. Thaw frozen cake overnight in the refrigerator.
